sanskrit_devanagari: अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् ।सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥ ३४ ॥
verse_text_iast: akīrtiṁ cāpi bhūtānikathayiṣyanti te ’vyayāmsambhāvitasya cākīrtirmaraṇād atiricyate
synonyms: akīrtim — infamy; ca — also; api — over and above; bhūtāni — all people; kathayiṣyanti — will speak; te — of you; avyayām — forever; sambhāvitasya — for a respectable man; ca — also; akīrtiḥ — ill fame; maraṇāt — than death; atiricyate — becomes more.
translation: People will always speak of your infamy, and for a respectable person, dishonor is worse than death.
purport_excerpt: Both as friend and philosopher to Arjuna, Lord Kṛṣṇa now gives His final judgment regarding Arjuna’s refusal to fight. The Lord says, “Arjuna, if you leave the battlefield before the battle even begins, people will call you a coward.
